checks.a_analysis: The rendered HTML faithfully follows the visual organization of the source PDF. The document is structured logically with a table of contents and sections for each vendor, mirroring the multi-page layout of the PDF (Broadcom/Symantec, Sophos, Trellix/McAfee, CrowdStrike, and Cisco). Tables are placed correctly within their respective sections.

All tables across the various vendor sections are correctly rendered. Columns align properly, borders are visible where appropriate, and headers are clearly distinguished from body rows. Complex tables with grouped headers (e.g., the Sophos 'Good', 'Better', 'Best' tiers) are handled correctly, maintaining the intended meaning for a sighted user.
The source PDF is a text and table-heavy price list document. No meaningful images (logos, diagrams, charts) are present in the source that are missing or broken in the HTML output.

A review of the HTML screenshot shows no instances of body content duplication. Each table and descriptive block appears exactly once in the sequence defined by the source PDF. The presence of section titles in the table of contents as well as the body headings is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute content duplication.
